results.title: NPPA Revises Coronary Stent Prices, DES at Rs 39,186 and BMS at Rs 10,762 from April 2026
results.content:
# NPPA Revises Coronary Stent Prices, DES at Rs 39,186 and BMS at Rs 10,762 from April 2026

Written By : Susmita Roy   |

New Delhi: Through a recent notice, the National Pharmaceutical Pricing Authority (NPPA), under the Department of Pharmaceuticals, Ministry of Chemicals and Fertilizers, has notified revised ceiling prices for coronary stents, factoring in the Wholesale Price Index (WPI) increase of 0.64956% for the year 2025 over 2024.

The revised ceiling prices of coronary stents have been fixed at ₹10,762.15 per unit for Bare Metal Stents (BMS), while Drug Eluting Stents (DES), including metallic DES and Bioresorbable Vascular Scaffolds (BVS)/biodegradable stents, have been priced at ₹39,186.03 per unit.These prices are applicable per stent and are exclusive of GST. [...] prices of Coronary Stents as mentioned in column no. (4) in the table below, exclusive of Goods and Services Tax as applicable, and unit specified in column (3) with effect from 01.04.2026, as under: [...] Further, the notification added,

(a) All the existing manufacturers/importers of Coronary Stents having MRP lower than the ceiling price specified in column (4) in the above table (plus Goods and Services Taxes as applicable, if any), may revise the existing MRP of Coronary Stent, on the basis of WPI @ 0.64956% for the year 2025 over 2024 in accordance with Paragraph 16(2) of DPCO, 2013, read with Para 13(2) of DPCO, 2013.

(b) The manufacturers/ importers of Coronary Stents may add Goods and Services Taxes only if they have paid actually or if it is payable to the Government on the ceiling price mentioned in column (4) of the aforesaid table.

results.title: Cardiac stent price capped by NPPA
results.content:
Minister of State for Chemicals and Fertilizers Smt. Anupriya Patel provided the details in a written reply in the Lok Sabha on February 13. She stated that the National Pharmaceutical Pricing Authority (NPPA) fixes ceiling prices for coronary stents, including Bare Metal Stents (BMS) and Drug Eluting Stents (DES), under Schedule-I of DPCO, 2013.

At present, the ceiling cardiac stent price for BMS stands at ₹10,692.69, while DES carries a ceiling of ₹38,933.14. All manufacturers, marketers and importers must sell stents within these limits, plus applicable Goods and Services Tax. [...] In addition, NPPA issued a notification on February 12, 2018. The order mandates that hospitals, nursing homes and clinics performing angioplasty must comply with the notified ceiling rates when billing patients directly. Institutions must also separately mention the stent cost, category, brand name, manufacturer or importer, batch number and specifications in bills and estimates.

## Regulation and enforcement of cardiac stent price

The Minister noted that health is a State subject under the Constitution. Therefore, State Governments and Union Territories must act against cases where private hospitals charge excessive amounts. [...] Likes

# NPPA fixes ceiling price for cardiac stents

By NA Web Desk

Cardiac stent price capped by NPPA

New Delhi: The Union Government said the cardiac stent price remains regulated under the Drugs (Prices Control) Order, 2013, with fixed ceiling rates applicable across the country.

results.title: NPPA revises coronary stent prices for 2026: What Patients Need to Know | Dailyhunt
results.content:
The NPPA has also directed retailers and medical device dealers, including hospital pharmacies, to prominently display the new price list on their premises so that it is easily accessible to the public. The private hospitals will be allowed to add GST only if they have actually paid it to the government, and it cannot be used as an arbitrary tool to inflate the base ceiling price. To prevent hidden costs, hospitals are also required to provide itemised billing that specifically mentions the cost of the stent, the brand name, and the manufacturer's details. [...] For a Bare Metal Stent (BMS), the new ceiling price has been fixed at Rs 10,762.15, while for the more commonly used Drug Eluting Stents (DES), the price is now capped at Rs 39,186.03. The higher category price of DES also covers advanced medical variants, including Metallic DES, Bioresorbable Vascular Scaffolds (BVS), and other biodegradable stents, the price notification released by NPPA said.

The notification includes strict directives to ensure transparency at the hospital level. Senior doctors and public health communicators in Hyderabad have urged patients and their family members to make themselves aware of the new price cap on stents. [...] Dailyhunt
NPPA revises coronary stent prices for 2026: What Patients Need to Know

# NPPA revises coronary stent prices for 2026: What Patients Need to Know

Hyderabad: To ensure the cost of life-saving medical devices remains within the reach of poor patients, the regulatory authorities have officially revised and fixed the ceiling prices of coronary stents in the country.

The National Pharmaceutical Pricing Authority (NPPA), the price regulatory authority for drugs in the country, has issued orders, fixing the cost of coronary stents. The NPPA has set specific price caps for different categories of stents. These prices are the maximum that a hospital or retailer can charge, exclusive of Goods and Services Tax (GST).

results.title: NPPA notified regarding the Revision of
results.content:
Industry Specific Compliance

The following has been stated namely: -

• Prices are updated based on Wholesale Price Index (WPI) increase of 0.64956% for 2025 over 2024.

• Bare Metal Stents (BMS) ceiling price is fixed at ₹10,762.15 per unit (excluding GST).

• Drug Eluting Stents (DES) including metallic DES and bioresorbable/biodegradable stents are capped at ₹39,186.03 per unit (excluding GST).

• Manufacturers with MRPs below the ceiling price may revise prices upward only as per WPI rules under DPCO 2013.

• GST can be added only if actually paid or payable to the government.

• Retailers and dealers must display updated price lists prominently for public access. [...] Chemical Industry
